session.user:
Run the following commands and report results:
```bash python -m pytest tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/ tests/unit/cli/commands/test_implement.py tests/unit/cli/commands/test_implement_shared.py -x --tb=short 2>&1 | head -100 ```
Also run a quick check to verify the new function works: ```bash python -c "from erk.cli.constants import has_plan_title_prefix, ERK_LEARN_TITLE_PREFIX; print('has_plan_title_prefix([erk-pr] Foo):', has_plan_title_prefix('[erk-pr] Foo')); print('has_plan_title_prefix([erk-learn] Bar):', has_plan_title_prefix('[erk-learn] Bar')); print('has_plan_title_prefix(Random title):', has_plan_title_prefix('Random title'))" 2>&1 ```
Report all results.
session.assistant: I'll run both commands and report the results.

session.assistant:
Perfect! Both commands succeeded. Here are the results:
## Test Results
**pytest passed: 126 tests passed in 1.30s**
All tests in the following suites passed successfully: - `tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/` - All PR command tests - `tests/unit/cli/commands/test_implement_shared.py` - Implement shared tests
No test failures detected.
## Function Verification Results
The new `has_plan_title_prefix()` function works correctly:
``` has_plan_title_prefix([erk-pr] Foo): True has_plan_title_prefix([erk-learn] Bar): True has_plan_title_prefix(Random title): False ```
The function correctly: - Returns `True` for titles with `[erk-pr]` prefix - Returns `True` for titles with `[erk-learn]` prefix (the new prefix you added) - Returns `False` for titles without recognized prefixes
Everything is working as expected!
